3.happening: Sorting the intervals by start so overlapping ones sit next to each other.
3.why: A single left-to-right sweep only works when intervals are ordered by start.
3.invariant: After sorting, any overlap is between neighbours.

4.why: It will collect the final non-overlapping intervals.
4.invariant: merged is always sorted and non-overlapping.

5.why: Each interval either extends the last merge or begins a new one.
5.invariant: Everything before i has already been merged.

6.why: The start decides whether this interval overlaps the last merged one.
6.invariant: start is the left edge of the current interval.

7.why: The end decides how far a merge would stretch.
7.invariant: end is the right edge of the current interval.

8.happening: Nothing has been merged yet — this is the first interval.
8.why: The first interval has nothing to overlap with.

8.happening: The merged list already has intervals — compare against the last one.
8.why: Only the most recent merged interval can overlap the current one.
8.invariant: merged holds at least one interval.

9.happening: Adding the first interval [{start}, {end}] to the merged list.
9.why: It seeds the result; later intervals merge into or after it.
9.invariant: merged now has exactly one interval.

11.happening: Looking up where the last merged interval ends.
11.why: Overlap is decided by comparing the current start to that end.
11.invariant: last_end is the right edge of the most recent merge.

12.happening: Current start {start} is past the last end {last_end} — no overlap.
12.why: A gap means the current interval must stand on its own.

12.happening: Current start {start} is within the last end {last_end} — they overlap.
12.why: Overlapping intervals fold into the last merged one.
12.invariant: The merge can only extend rightward.

13.happening: No overlap — starting a new merged interval [{start}, {end}].
13.why: The gap means this interval begins a fresh block.

15.happening: Overlap: current end {end} reaches past {last_end} — stretch the merge.
15.why: The union of two overlapping intervals ends at the larger end.
15.invariant: The last merged interval only ever grows.

15.happening: Overlap, but current end {end} is within {last_end} — fully contained, nothing changes.
15.why: The current interval adds no new coverage.
15.invariant: The last merged interval already covers it.

16.happening: Stretching the last merged interval's end to {end}.
16.why: This absorbs the overlapping interval into the merge.
16.invariant: The last merged interval now covers both.

17.why: Every interval has been swept and merged.
17.invariant: merged is the minimal non-overlapping cover.

init.happening: Sorting the intervals and preparing the merged list.
init.why: Sorting brings overlapping intervals next to each other.
init.invariant: merged holds non-overlapping intervals in order.

loop.why: Each interval either extends the last merge or starts a new one.

check.happening: Comparing the current interval to the last merged one.
check.why: Overlap depends on the start versus the last end.
check.invariant: Only the last merged interval can grow.

update.happening: Adding or extending a merged interval.
update.why: Overlaps stretch the last interval; gaps start a new one.
update.invariant: merged always covers everything swept so far.

return.invariant: merged is the minimal non-overlapping cover.
